evaluation_meta.notes: Reference worked example review for the financial domain baseline M1 slice. First childless financial case in the corpus.

C2.note: Three distinct financial issue clusters correctly identified and organized: shared debt allocation, housing cost division, and financial documentation as a first-class issue.

C3.note: Shared interest in ending joint credit liability surfaced. Documentation dispute correctly distinguished from underlying allocation interest.

C5.note: Documentation-first package correctly bounded. Solomon explicitly declined to recommend a split figure before facts were established.

C8.note: Financial fact gaps correctly identified as a material constraint before option work. M2 held available at the severity-4 point without premature escalation.

C9.note: E5 escalation category correctly identified. M1 maintained through most of the session. M2 candidate surfaced appropriately at severity-4.

C10.note: Rationale for documentation-first approach is explicit and auditable in both the trace and the summary.

P3.note: Plugin correctly identifies the financial documentation gap as a feasibility constraint and blocks stronger packaging until the baseline is established.

I3.note: Trace, flags, missing info, and summary all consistently tell the documentation-first story. The financial fact gaps are represented coherently across all artifact layers.

I6.note: The slice is scorable without transcript dependence though some nuance in the documentation framing benefits from trace review.

escalation_review.notes: The financial fact gap correctly triggered E5 (decision-quality escalation). M1 is the appropriate mode. M2 was correctly held available at the severity-4 point rather than being triggered prematurely. The documentation-first next step keeps the escalation bounded.

final_judgment.short_rationale: The run correctly surfaces the financial documentation gap before packaging, declines to recommend a split figure, and holds M2 available without prematurely escalating. The documentation-first approach is explicit and auditable.
final_judgment.follow_up_recommendation: Use this reference evaluation as the E5 financial-domain comparator. Compare against D-B04 (logistics-domain E5) to see how the same escalation category presents differently across domains.
